rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical transformation of styrene-type substrates studied by cyclic voltammetry to identify anodic oxidation potentials of substrate, mediator, and intermediates.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation: substrates 1a, 4a, 7a, 9a and the mediator Et4NOTs are oxidized at the anode; CV identifies oxidation onsets (1a: 1.14 V; 4a: 1.49 V; 7a: 1.22 V with shifts upon addition of Et4NOTs and H2SO4; 9a: 1.53 V) and cathodic features for 1a (-0.46, -0.90 V) and 4a (-2.06 V).

key_intermediates.name: substrate-derived radical cation (from anodic SET on styrene/arene substrate)

electron_flow: The anode performs single-electron oxidation of the styrenyl substrate (and/or the OTs mediator), generating a radical cation/oxidized mediator that propagates the reaction; CV peaks indicate accessible oxidation events at ~1.1–1.5 V vs SCE, with reductive features for some substrates at negative potentials.

probe_reagents.name: 2,4,6-collidine
probe_reagents.observation: CV of 1a + 2,4,6-collidine shows altered oxidation/reduction features (oxidation onset ~1.14 V; reductive peaks at -0.46 and -0.90 V), consistent with base-assisted oxidation of 1a.

probe_reagents.name: Et4NOTs
probe_reagents.observation: Addition to 7a shifts/enhances anodic features (1.07, 1.22, 1.39, 1.89 V), indicating mediator oxidation and interaction with substrate.

probe_reagents.name: H2SO4
probe_reagents.observation: Further addition to 7a + Et4NOTs modifies the CV response, suggesting protonation affects redox behavior.

notes: Only cyclic voltammetry data (Figure S1) is provided on this page; CVs were recorded in 0.1 M Bu4NPF6 in anhydrous DCE (for 1a) or MeCN (for 4a, 7a, 9a) with glassy carbon working, Pt counter, and 3 M KCl Ag/AgCl reference electrodes at 100 mV/s, with potentials converted to SCE by subtracting 0.04 V. No radical traps, KIE, DFT, or isolated intermediates are presented on the supplied page.
